# [LLVM] Crash in UpgradeIntrinsicCall - "Use still stuck around after Def is destroyed" for llvm.x86.sse2.pshuflw

**Author:** sairam2661
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/176674
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** crash, llvm:ir, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2026-02-05T10:17:54Z

## Body

**Fuzzer Generated Test** 
**Test Commit**
[0b2f3cfb72a76fa90f3ec2a234caabe0d0712590](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/0b2f3cfb72a76fa90f3ec2a234caabe0d0712590)

**Description**
The intrinsic upgrade logic crashes when `llvm.x86.sse2.pshuflw` is called with `<4 x i32>` instead of the expected `<8 x i16>`. It attempts to erase the old call while uses still exist.

**Steps to reproduce**
- Minimized test case, `input.ll`
```
define void @test(ptr %a) {
  %v = call <4 x i32> @llvm.x86.sse2.pshuflw.128(<4 x i32> zeroinitializer, i8 0)
  store <4 x i32> %v, ptr %a
  ret void
}
```

**Command**
```
opt -passes=verify -S input.ll
```

**Output**
```
While deleting: <4 x i32> %v
Use still stuck around after Def is destroyed:  store <4 x i32> %v, ptr %a, align 16
Uses remain when a value is destroyed!
UNREACHABLE executed at /workdir/llvm-project/llvm/lib/IR/Value.cpp:102!
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace and instructions to reproduce the bug.
Stack dump:
0.      Program arguments: opt -passes=verify -S input.ll
```
